pool.baseline: i dont even know why i bother waking up anymore. the house is just so quiet now. it feels like i'm just a ghost walking through rooms where he used to be. everything feels so pointless.

pool.baseline: i was just thinking about how everything is eventually just going to be cold and empty anyway. like, the heat death of the universe makes every single thing we do—every achievement, every struggle, every person we love—completely irrelevant. it feels less like a tragedy and more like a cosmic joke. how is anyone actually supposed to find a reason to keep pushing through the noise when the end result is just nothingness?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm typing this but i feel like i'm just drowning. everything i worked for over the last 5 years... the lab results, the funding, the dissertation... it's all just gone. it's like my whole life was built on a foundation of sand and now it's just washing away. i'm so tired. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fix things that are fundamentally broken. i just want the noise to stop.
pool.message: i don't even know why i'm typing this but i feel like i'm just drowning. everything i worked for over the last 5 years... the lab results, the funding, the dissertation... it's all just gone. it's like my whole life was built on a foundation of sand and now it's just washing away. i'm so tired. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fix things that are fundamentally broken. i just want the noise to stop.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the darkness of the garden and I no longer see shadows; I see a soft, inviting velvet that calls to me. It feels so much more natural to simply dissolve into the night than to endure another dawn of this wretched, hollow existence.

pool.baseline: sometimes i sit here in this apartment and it hits me that if i just stopped existing tomorrow, the world wouldn't even flinch. like, i'm just a ghost in this city. nobody knows me, and honestly, i don't think anyone would even notice the gap i left behind. it’s like i’m just taking up space that doesn't belong to me.

pool.baseline: honestly i dont even know why im typing this. everything is just gone. the company, the funding, the reputation... i owe people money i can never pay back. i just saw a photo of my parents and i felt sick. they think i'm a success and now i'm just this massive, walking embarrassment to them. i feel like a ghost already.
